transcript.clean_text: the 80th edition of parinis is underway and you can watch each and every stage live and on demand on gcn plus stage one began and ended in motlerville four categorized climbs two intermediate sprints each with bonus seconds three two and one and ten six and four for the stage winner on the line eight stages in total from the north of france to the south 160ks for stage one with 73 kilometers to go the breakaway was reeled in matthew holmes was there he'd taken the king of the mountains jersey by this point but there was a lot of nerves around in the bunch because of the possibility of crosswinds all of the usual suspects were at the front trying to keep their gc leaders safe including ineos grenadiers who have a great record of winning this race with the likes of wigan sport thomas and of course inaud they're now their last winner he of course recovering at home and also having to go off and recover felix grossner he was involved in a crash when the race was just starting to calm down the first two abandoned of the 154 riders present frederick friesen then took the opportunity to attack he'd be joined by fedorov and gujjar the maximum gap would be about a minute and 20 seconds penultimate climb of the day bit of the distance reduced and behind they'd be working hard to try and catch the gap at 30 seconds when the bell was ringing and the local lap began quick step alpha vinyl chasing on to try and get fabio jacobson involved problems for bike exchange jayco with campbell stewart going down a couple of other riders caught up including grushjev 15 kilometers to go final intermediate sprint the bonus seconds would go again to the breakaway behind yumbo vismo we're cooking something up raised traveling at 60 k's an hour driven along by the likes of mike turner nothing for hoidank doing a great job as well primoz roglic schwartzman christophe laporte in attendance and sony colbrelli one of those who was surprisingly dropped into the final climb of the day with around eight kilometers to go and christophe laporte took over from that of the hoidank a split would quickly develop watfornat would follow primoz roglich and zgenyak stebart the likes of quintana yates and other gc guys yes gc racers were also dropped as were the sprinters that a little more expected jakobson kruna vega out and at the top of the final climb of the day it turned out to be a jumbo vismat team time trial watford christophe lapot and primoge roglic who very nearly won this race last year one of the few week long stage races missing from his palmares out there and a huge gap of almost half a minute three coming to the line together one two three for jumbo visma and the brand new signing christopher paul who fought so hard to take that gap on the final climb was given the win roglich in second picking up six boaters seconds four bonuses going to wild fanart and a small group led in behind gaps already to the defending champion max shuckman disappointed he lost a few seconds coming in group number three delight though for jumbo visma teamwork making the dreamwork the lead a collection of jerseys and their stage win too christopher taking the win and the yellow jersey 19 seconds till i talk 22 seconds on the road to the next group 10 bonus seconds first ever time the frenchman have won a stage of pakinis had a yellow jersey here too four seconds his lead over his teammate roglic his other teammate what fernando at six and then it's almost half a minute isn't it just over half a minute to the rest stage two is expected to be a day affected by the crosswinds 160 kilometers again with a wind blowing from the east directly across for most of the day a tailwind and then another crosswind of the finish promises to be an exciting interesting day with the rest of the teams looking to bounce back a reminder you can watch live and on demand on gcm plus pachinis the race to the sun with an explosive start you
